full_prompt: Design the primary chassis frame of a two-person tandem human-powered planetary rover benchmark. Rover folds into a 5 ft x 5 ft x 5 ft (1.52 m x 1.52 m x 1.52 m) stowed cube at the start line, then unfolds to a deployed configuration for the course. Maximum empty rover mass (without crew) <= 113 kg (250 lb) per benchmark handbook. Two riders sit in tandem, each with harness-restrained seating and independent pedaling drivetrain to the ground wheels. Wheelbase in deployed configuration: 2.1 m, track width: 1.3 m, with four independently-sprung wheels each rated for an obstacle impact of 3 g over a 30 cm berm. Frame material: 6061-T6 aluminum tubing (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), primary tubes 38 mm OD x 1.6 mm wall. Course obstacles per benchmark handbook: 8 terrain features including crater edges, dune traverse at 10 deg slope, boulder field requiring ground clearance 20 cm, plus four tool-task stations where the rover stops and the crew operates on-board sampling arms. Load cases: (LC1) dual-crew loading: 2 x 85 kg = 170 kg crew + 113 kg rover = 283 kg total, 1 g static; (LC2) 30 cm berm crossing: 3 g vertical impulse on one wheel; (LC3) lateral slope: 10 deg side slope with full 283 kg weight, 0.17 g lateral; (LC4) pedaling reaction: 600 N horizontal at each pedal drive at 1.5 Hz for the 900-second course (1350 cycles per pedal); (LC5) tool-task reaction: 500 N vertical + 300 N horizontal at the tool-mount bracket on the frame during sampling; (LC6) stow-cube compliance: frame must fold into 5x5x5 ft cube without plastic deformation at hinge joints. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in aluminum tubes under LC1-LC5 <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5); (R2) wheel-axle deflection under LC2 berm <= 10 mm; (R3) hinge-joint tube stress under fold/deploy cycle below 184 MPa; (R4) empty rover mass <= 113 kg (250 lb); (R5) first torsional frame mode above 10 Hz; (R6) stowed-cube envelope compliance verified geometrically. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per case, modal, mass report, and stow-cube compliance check.
short_prompt:
Design the primary chassis frame of a two-person tandem human-powered planetary rover that folds into a 5 ft (1.52 m) stowed cube at the start line and unfolds in the deployed configuration. Two riders sit in tandem, each with harness restraint and an independent pedaling drivetrain; four independently-sprung wheels clear a 30 cm berm under 3 g. Material class: 6061-T6 aluminum tubing (nominal 38 mm OD x 1.6 mm wall). No external standard is named in the source; design to a 1.5 FoS on yield.

Envelope: stowed 1.52 m cube; deployed wheelbase 2.1 m, track 1.3 m, ground clearance >= 20 cm; empty rover mass <= 113 kg (250 lb).

Load cases.
- LC1 dual-crew static: 2 x 85 kg + 113 kg = 283 kg at 1 g.
- LC2 berm: 3 g vertical impulse on one wheel over a 30 cm berm.
- LC3 side slope: 0.17 g lateral (10 deg) with 283 kg.
- LC4 pedaling reaction: 600 N horizontal at each pedal drive at 1.5 Hz, 1350 cycles per pedal over the 900 s course.
- LC5 tool-task reaction: 500 N vertical + 300 N horizontal at the tool-mount bracket during sampling.
- LC6 stow-cube fold/deploy: no plastic deformation at hinge joints through the fold cycle.

prompt.title: Human-powered planetary rover frame
prompt.brief: Two-crew human-powered rover frame folding into 5x5x5 ft stowed cube at 113 kg empty.
